title: [DRAFT] Merge DFF cells connected to BRAM output ports into BRAM cell, preserving its functionality
body:
Derived from Litex VexRiscv+Ethernet SoC, registered outputs w/wo CE and/or RST functionallity in BRAM cells, can be merged into that BRAM, reducing the number of flip-flop cells that the synthesis infers and thus, increasing QoR:

__Before xilinx_bram pass, 36 FDRE.__
```
=== blockram ===
     FDRE                           36
     LUT2                           37
     RAMB36E1                        1
```

__After xilinx_bram pass, 0 FDRE.__
```
=== blockram ===
     LUT2                            1
     RAMB36E1                        1
```
In the latter case, the BRAM DOx_REG (where x is A or B) is set to 1, and the flops with CE or RST functionallity, are connected to the corresponding BRAM input.

---

There exist also test cases in `tests/arch/xilinx/xilinx_bram.ys` that demonstrate:

* [1] Vivado's suggested Verilog for BRAM with CE and RST.
* [2] Test case [1] increasing address and data width (matching shuffled DOA/DOB bits).
* [3] Reduced test case extracted from Litex VexRiscv+Ethernet SoC (2 BRAMS).
* [4] Original test case extracted from Litex VexRiscv+Ethernet SoC (4 BRAMS).

All of the pattern match code is heavily inherited from `xilinx_dsp.pmg`.

__TODO__:
Process other BRAMs that are not `RAMB18E1` and `RAMB36E1`.
